In [3]:
import os
import spotipy
from spotipy.oauth2 import SpotifyOAuth

username = os.environ.get("SPOTIFY_USERNAME","")
client_id = os.environ["SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID"]
client_secret = os.environ["SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET"]
redirect_uri = 'https://example.org/callabck'

scope = 'user-read-recently-played'
sp = spotipy.Spotify(auth_manager=SpotifyOAuth(client_id=client_id,
                                               client_secret=client_secret,
                                               redirect_uri=redirect_uri,
                                               scope=scope,
                                               username=username,
                                               open_browser=False))
print(sp.me())
In [5]:
import os
import spotipy
import spotipy.util as util
from spotipy.oauth2 import SpotifyClientCredentials
cid =os.environ["SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID"]
secret = os.environ["SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET"]

client_credentials_manager = SpotifyClientCredentials(client_id=cid, client_secret=secret)
sp = spotipy.Spotify(client_credentials_manager=client_credentials_manager)
In [8]:
sp = spotipy.Spotify(auth_manager=SpotifyOAuth(client_id=client_id,
                                               client_secret=client_secret,
                                               redirect_uri=redirect_uri,
                                               scope=scope,
                                               username=username,
                                               open_browser=False))
recently_played = sp.current_user_recently_played()
for track in recently_played['items']:
    print("Track:", track['track']['name'])
    print("Artist:", track['track']['artists'][0]['name'])
    print("Album:", track['track']['album']['name'])
    print("---")

In [ ]:
# Define  columns
selected_columns <- c('year', 'bpm', 'nrgy', 'dnce', 'dB', 'live', 'val', 'dur', 'acous', 'spch', 'decade')

# Initialize  empty list
conditional_probabilities <- list()

# Iterate through columns
for (column in selected_columns) {
  # column conditions
  column_condition <- df[[column]] > median(df[[column]])
  popular_condition <- df[['pop']] > median(df[['pop']])

  # number of tracks satisfying both conditions
  column_and_popular <- sum(column_condition & popular_condition)

  # total number of tracks
  total_tracks <- nrow(df)

  # conditional probability
  conditional_probability <- column_and_popular / total_tracks

  # Store result in list
  conditional_probabilities[[column]] <- conditional_probability
}


for (column in names(conditional_probabilities)) {
  cat(sprintf("Conditional Probability of being Popular given %s above median: %.2f%%\n", column, conditional_probabilities[[column]] * 100))
}
